name: Oslo (Norway)

colors.symbolism: the field of the city arms

description: A blue field bearing the figure of Saint Hallvard enthroned, holding three arrows and a millstone, with a nude female figure at his feet, all rendered in heraldic gold, white, and red.
history: Oslo is unique in Norway for being simultaneously a municipality (kommune) and a county (fylke). The flag, like the coat of arms, depicts Saint Hallvard, who has been the city's patron and emblem since the medieval period; the present design was officially adopted in 1924 (by royal decree of 17 December) based on the historical seal.

features.symbolism: the heraldic banner of the Oslo coat of arms: Saint Hallvard, patron of the city, enthroned with millstone and arrows above a naked woman, all on a blue field

features.role: Saint Hallvard, patron saint of Oslo
features.symbolism: Saint Hallvard Vebjornsson, the city's patron saint, depicted enthroned holding three arrows and a millstone, as he was killed defending an innocent woman; the woman lies at his feet
